text.title: Niche Positioning for Solo Dev Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of Niche Positioning for Solo Dev MUST enforce. Rationale + source per rule. Rules referenced by id from `content/06-decision-tree.xml`.

rule.statement: Lane MUST name vertical + stack + outcome. Two-axis lanes (e.g., 'Stripe for SaaS') are too broad and rejected.
rule.rationale: Two-axis lanes do not collapse market enough to be discoverable; three-axis lanes survive SEO + LinkedIn + referral search.

rule.statement: Lane choice MUST cite >=3 prior projects matching the lane. Aspirational lanes without history are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Positioning without lived evidence reads as marketing fluff; specific past projects are the moat.

rule.statement: Lane MUST pass the 12-word headline test ('I help X do Y by Z'). Headlines >12 words signal lane is still too broad.
rule.rationale: Headlines >12 words fail LinkedIn bio + cold-email subject + SEO meta description; downstream marketing collapses.

rule.statement: Operator MUST refuse >=1 non-fit project per quarter to validate the lane. Zero refusals = lane not enforced.
rule.rationale: Positioning that survives only when no temptation appears is not positioning; saying no is the test.

rule.statement: Decision record MUST list exit criteria (e.g., '6 months, < 3 inbound from lane => repivot'). Lanes without exits are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Solo operators overstay misfit lanes due to sunk-cost; defined exits force re-evaluation at known thresholds.

rule.statement: If applicability preconditions are unmet (see AGENTS.md Skip If), the methodology MUST be skipped and a one-line skip-reason logged.
rule.rationale: A named skip terminal keeps decision-tree leaves resolvable and prevents silent misuse on misfit inputs.
